steps.name: Test infrastructure: drivers and DSL scaffolding
steps.description: Create WebContext, ServerDriver, HTTPDriver, RepoDriver, GitHubStubDriver, and DSL scaffolding (setup.go, step.go, assertions.go). No acceptance test -- infrastructure only.
acceptance_criteria: WebContext compiles with server URL, HTTP client, cookies, last response fields
ServerDriver compiles and starts kanban-web binary as subprocess
HTTPDriver provides GET, POST, HTML parsing with cookie jar
RepoDriver creates temp git repos with .kanban/tasks/ structure
GitHubStubDriver stubs GET /user for token validation
quality_gates: All driver and DSL packages compile
steps.implementation_notes: Create tests/acceptance/backend/dsl/ and tests/acceptance/backend/driver/ packages. ServerDriver compiles cmd/kanban-web binary. HTTPDriver uses net/http with cookiejar. RepoDriver uses git init + t.TempDir(). GitHubStubDriver uses httptest.NewServer. cmd/kanban-web/main.go can be a minimal stub that compiles.

steps.name: Hello World: server starts and responds
steps.description: kanban-web binary starts an HTTP server and responds to board requests. Walking skeleton.
acceptance_criteria: Server starts and board page is reachable
Board response is visible (not error page)
steps.implementation_notes: Implement minimal server.go with /board route returning basic HTML. Wire in cmd/kanban-web/main.go. DSL steps: IVisitTheBoard, BoardIsVisible.

steps.name: Hello World: response time
steps.description: Board response loads within 500ms threshold.
acceptance_criteria: Board page loads within 500ms
steps.implementation_notes: DSL step: BoardLoadsWithin. Likely passes already if server is minimal. If not, profile and optimize.

steps.name: Board: three columns with tasks
steps.description: Board displays Todo, Doing, Done columns with tasks placed by status. First real use of domain layer.
acceptance_criteria: Board renders three columns: Todo, Doing, Done
Tasks appear in the column matching their status
Multiple tasks distributed across columns correctly
steps.implementation_notes: Implement board handler calling GetBoard use case. Create board.html template with three columns. Wire RepoDriver to seed tasks. DSL steps: ARepoWithTasks, ColumnContainsCards.

steps.name: Board: card shows summary info
steps.description: Clicking a card shows summary information including title, assignee, and status.
acceptance_criteria: Card displays title, assignee, and status
steps.implementation_notes: DSL steps: IViewCard, CardShows. May need card_detail.html or expand board card rendering. This test uses IViewCard so it touches card detail view.

steps.name: Board: cards sorted by date
steps.description: Cards within a column are sorted by creation date (oldest first).
acceptance_criteria: Cards in same column sorted by created_at ascending
steps.implementation_notes: DSL step: CardAppearsBeforeInColumn. Sorting may already be handled by GetBoard use case or needs template-level ordering.

steps.name: Board: empty board shows columns
steps.description: Board with no tasks still renders three empty columns.
acceptance_criteria: Empty repo produces board with Todo, Doing, Done columns
All three columns are empty
steps.implementation_notes: DSL steps: ARepoWithNoTasks, BoardHasColumns, ColumnIsEmpty.

steps.name: Board: reflects changes from another user
steps.description: When a developer pushes a new task to the repo, a stakeholder viewing the board sees the update after sync.
acceptance_criteria: Task pushed by one user appears on board viewed by another user
steps.implementation_notes: DSL steps: Users, User().PushesNewTask, User().VisitsTheBoard. Requires background git pull sync to be implemented. RepoDriver must support pushing tasks externally.

steps.name: Board: unauthenticated user can view
steps.description: A first-time visitor with no authentication can view the public board.
acceptance_criteria: Unauthenticated user sees board with tasks
steps.implementation_notes: DSL step: AFirstTimeVisitor. Board GET route requires no auth. Should pass if no auth middleware blocks read paths.

steps.name: Card detail: full info display
steps.description: Card detail view shows all task fields including title, description, priority, assignee, created_by, and status.
acceptance_criteria: Card detail displays all populated fields
All field values match the task file content
steps.implementation_notes: Expand card_detail.html to render all domain.Task fields. Handler uses TaskRepository.FindByID.

steps.name: Card detail: missing optional fields and task ID
steps.description: Card detail gracefully omits empty optional fields and always shows the task ID.
acceptance_criteria: Missing optional fields (assignee, priority) are not rendered
Task ID is always visible on card detail
steps.implementation_notes: Template uses conditional rendering for optional fields. DSL steps: CardDoesNotShow, CardShowsTaskID. Batched: both tests exercise the same template and handler with minimal variation.

steps.name: Auth: prompted on add task attempt
steps.description: Unauthenticated user attempting to add a task is redirected to the token entry form.
acceptance_criteria: First-time visitor clicking add task sees authentication prompt
Add task option is visible on the board (even when unauthenticated)
steps.implementation_notes: Implement auth middleware checking for encrypted cookie. Create token_entry.html. DSL steps: ITryToAddTask, PromptedToAuthenticate, AddTaskOptionIsVisible. Batched with UnauthenticatedCanViewNotAdd since both test unauthenticated add-task behavior.

steps.name: Auth: successful authentication
steps.description: User submits valid GitHub token and display name, receives encrypted cookie, and is redirected to the board with write access.
acceptance_criteria: Valid token + display name authenticates user
Authenticated user lands on the board
Authenticated user can add tasks
steps.implementation_notes: Implement POST /auth/token handler. Validate token against GitHubStubDriver. Set AES-256-GCM encrypted HttpOnly cookie. DSL steps: IAuthenticate, IAmOnTheBoard, ICanAddTasks.

steps.name: Auth: invalid credentials rejected
steps.description: User submitting an invalid GitHub token is rejected and cannot add tasks.
acceptance_criteria: Invalid token is rejected with clear feedback
Rejected user cannot add tasks
steps.implementation_notes: GitHubStubDriver returns 401 for invalid tokens. Handler returns error to token_entry.html. DSL steps: AnUnauthorizedUser, IAttemptToAuthenticate, AuthenticationIsRejected, ICannotAddTasks.

steps.name: Auth: session persistence
steps.description: Authenticated user retains access across subsequent requests without re-authenticating.
acceptance_criteria: Previously authenticated user can add tasks without re-entering token
steps.implementation_notes: Cookie persistence across requests. DSL steps: AnAuthenticatedUser, AddTaskFormIsShown. Should pass if cookie handling is correct.

steps.name: Add task: all fields via web form
steps.description: Authenticated user adds a task with title, description, priority, and assignee. Task appears on board and exists in repo.
acceptance_criteria: Task with all fields appears in Todo column after creation
Task file in repo contains all submitted fields plus created_by from auth
steps.implementation_notes: Create add_task.html form template. Implement POST /task handler calling AddTask use case. DSL steps: IAddTask, TaskExistsInRepo. Wire AddTask use case with TaskRepository in handler.

steps.name: Add task: title only with defaults
steps.description: Task created with only title gets sensible defaults (status: todo, created_by from auth).
acceptance_criteria: Title-only task appears in Todo column
Task file has status todo and created_by set from authenticated user
steps.implementation_notes: Handler applies defaults for missing optional fields. DSL verifies default values in repo.

steps.name: Add task: title required validation
steps.description: Submitting a task without a title fails with validation error and no task is created.
acceptance_criteria: Empty title produces validation error with 'title is required' message
No task file created in repo
steps.implementation_notes: Handler validates title before calling use case. Returns form with error message. DSL steps: TaskCreationFails, NoNewTaskInRepo.

steps.name: Add task: file format and sequential ID
steps.description: Created task file follows Markdown + YAML front matter format with a sequential task ID.
acceptance_criteria: Task file uses Markdown + YAML front matter format
Task ID follows sequential pattern (TASK-NNN)
steps.implementation_notes: DSL steps: TaskFileIsValidFormat, TaskHasSequentialID. Verify file structure matches ADR-002 format.

steps.name: Add task: committed and pushed to remote
steps.description: New task is git-committed and pushed to the remote repository.
acceptance_criteria: Task file committed to local repo
Commit pushed to remote repo
Task visible in remote repo
steps.implementation_notes: Implement AddTaskAndPush use case orchestrating AddTask + RemoteGitPort.Add/Commit/Push. DSL steps: ARepoWithRemote, RemoteRepoContainsTask. RepoDriver verifies bare remote contains the commit.

steps.name: Add task: unauthenticated rejected
steps.description: Direct POST to add task without authentication is rejected.
acceptance_criteria: Unauthenticated POST to add task is rejected
No task file created in repo
steps.implementation_notes: Auth middleware rejects POST /task without valid cookie. DSL steps: IAttemptToAddTaskDirectly, TaskCreationIsRejected, NoNewTaskInRepo.
